summary: Small rectangular single-storey building with brick walls, a stepped dark-red (nether-brick) hipped/stepped roof, stone-corner pillars and a white stone trim/band near roofline. Windows run along the long walls above a wooden baseboard; the whole sits inside a low white stone border on flattened dirt.

shape.overall_form: rectangular box with a centered stepped/hipped roof
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along the long center axis (roof steps mirrored)

materials.name: quartz
materials.role: trim / top band and outer low border

elements: continuous row windows on both long sides (3-4 panes each side)
brick walls with wooden lower strip
white horizontal trim band under roof all around
stepped dark-red roof made of horizontal slab/stair layers
outer low white stone border surrounding building on ground
rebuild_hints: Footprint is rectangular; walls are 1 block thick brick with a 1-block-high wooden planks strip at the bottom and a quartz (white) band 1 block high near top.
Place glass windows in a continuous row centered on the long walls between wood base and quartz band (windows appear 1 block high and repeated).
Corners use stacked stonebrick (pillar) blocks rising to the quartz band height.
Roof is built from nether_brick stairs/slabs in several nested horizontal steps forming a low hipped/stepped roof; add light-colored slabs at the four corner step ends.
Surround the building with a 1-block-high quartz border set on the ground; building sits in a shallow dirt plaza.
uncertainties: Exact block types for white trim and border are assumed quartz (could be another white stone like smooth stone); confidence 0.85.
Corner pillar material looks like stonebrick but could be polished/different stone variant; confidence 0.6.
Roof detail uses nether_brick stairs/slabs but exact orientation (stairs vs full blocks) is inferred from stepped appearance; confidence 0.9.
Width/depth/height integers are estimated from visible block counts in images and may be off by ±1-2 blocks.
